summary: C++ template instantiation error during build: the code attempts to explicitly instantiate `accumulate_moments_simd::operator()` with `xsimd::neon64`, but the template is defined for `xsimd::neon`, causing a type mismatch.
root_cause: Mismatch between explicit template instantiation and template definition: line 255 of pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp attempts to instantiate the template with `xsimd::neon64`, but the actual template definition at line 182 is defined for `xsimd::neon`, not `xsimd::neon64`. The function signatures do not match.

evidence.quote: error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member
evidence.reason: Core compiler error indicating the template instantiation is invalid

evidence.quote: could not match 'Moments (xsimd::neon64, std::span<const double>, int, std::optional<std::span<const uint8_t>>, int) noexcept' (aka 'Moments (xsimd::neon64, span<const double>, int, optional<span<const unsigned char>>, int) noexcept') against 'Moments (xsimd::neon, std::span<const double>, int, std::optional<std::span<const uint8_t>>, int)'
evidence.reason: Explicit statement of the signature mismatch: template instantiation uses neon64 but definition uses neon
The explicit instantiation line (255) that attempts to use neon64
The actual template definition (line 182) that is defined for xsimd::neon, not xsimd::neon64
Build failure in NEON SIMD compilation unit

suggested_fix: Review and correct the template instantiation in pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp line 255 to match the actual template definition, ensuring the template parameter (xsimd::neon vs xsimd::neon64) and function signature are consistent between the declaration and instantiation.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
